citations.narrative:
On two separate occasions, 3/10/2025 and 9/24/2024, a Fat-tailed dunnart escaped from its enclosure during a routine
cage change. In both cases during attempted recapture the dunnart was injured and died.
Handling (including approved protocol procedures and routine procedures) if not done as carefully as possible can cause
injury, trauma, stress, illness and death to the animal.
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma,
overheating, excessive cooling, behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort.
Program implemented corrections at time of occurrence, these were verified at time of inspection: 02/24/2026
This inspection was conducted on 02/24/2026 and 02/25/2026 and the exit interview was conducted on 02/25/2026 with
facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.38(f)(1)  Critical
   
Miscellaneous. 
On two separate occasions, 3/10/2025 and 9/24/2024, a Fat-tailed dunnart escaped from its enclosure during a routine 
cage change. In both cases during attempted recapture the dunnart was injured and died.  
Handling (including approved protocol procedures and routine procedures) if not done as carefully as possible can cause 
injury, trauma, stress, illness and death to the animal. 
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma, 
overheating, excessive cooling, behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort. 
Program implemented corrections at time of occurrence, these were verified at time of inspection: 02/24/2026 
 
This inspection was conducted on 02/24/2026 and 02/25/2026 and the exit interview was conducted on 02/25/2026 with 
facility representatives.
